title: Cloud LTX warm-speed: warm-on-connect + remove MpiClearVram node 58 (stage-2 re-fault)
description: Follow-up to MPI-156 perf resolution (2026-06-28). The cloud LTX cold-load tax (~3:30 first gen) is a one-time per-session cost; warm gens are fast (36s 1s/704p, beats local). ONE optimization remains. (1) WARM-ON-CONNECT (priority, biggest UX win, app-side only, no rebuild) -- STILL OPEN: right after a Pod connects + ComfyUI is ready, fire a tiny throwaway LTX gen in the background so the 40GB transformer is resident BEFORE the user kicks their first real gen -> they never see the ~3:30 cold fault, their first gen is already ~36s. Likely wire into the connect/ready path in routes/remoteProxy.js + comfyController; reuse an existing minimal LTX workflow at lowest res/1-frame; discard the output. (2) REMOVE MpiClearVram node 58 -- DISPROVEN LIVE 2026-06-28 (5090 Pod), DO NOT SHIP. The hypothesis was backwards. Bypassed node 58 in comfy_workflows/LTX_t2v.json (rewired stage-1-latent consumers 104+126 from node-58 to node-52, orphaning 58) and ran a warm t2v 1s/1K gen: 119.42s vs the 45s baseline (2.6x SLOWER), first stage-2 sampler step = 69 s/it. aimdo re-stages the 40GB at stage-2 REGARDLESS of node 58 (log printed 40050MB Staged, 0 patches even with 58 orphaned; VRAM still dropped between stages). Node 58's unload_all_models() actually EVICTS stage-1 VRAM so the stage-2 fault lands on a clean slate; without it the stage-2 fault thrashes against stage-1 resident pages. So node 58 is load-bearing for SPEED, not the cause of the transition pause. Wan's 3 MpiClearVram nodes serve the same role. The LTX_t2v.json edit was reverted (clean against HEAD). Keep node 58 in all 4 LTX workflows + the template. ALSO still untested: transition LoRA (node 192, Input_Use_Transition, OFF by default) may give more movement -> separate quality toggle test. Full context: docs/builder/research/pod-perf-investigation.md (top banner). BRANCH RunPod, mpi-ci separate, builds/pushes user-gated.
